summary: Tested Wall Board: backend pytest 19/19 passed (auth, users, wall/settings, goals, countdown, achievements, notes, family-events, photos, events). Frontend E2E with Playwright at 412x915: login with FAMILY2026 -> WallBoard at /; hero edit, message-of-the-day, goals add/toggle/delete, countdown add, family-events add, notes add, settings dialog (sync-now + two-tap logout), bottom nav with all 5 testids, /wall-board and /dashboard redirects to /, and full persistence after page reload — all working.

issues: React a11y console warning: 'Missing Description or aria-describedby for DialogContent' — add DialogDescription to these dialogs for screen-reader support (low priority)

action_items: (Optional, low priority) Add DialogDescription to Goal/Note/FamilyEvent/Countdown/Achievement editor dialogs to silence React a11y warnings.

(Optional, low priority) Consider showing a brief skeleton loader on first WallBoard mount — initial render shows empty-state cards for ~1-2 sec before server data fills in (because localStorage cache is empty on a fresh device).
critical_code_review_comments: wallApi.js uses optimistic local UUIDs for creates that race with server-assigned UUIDs. The flushQueue/cached refresh works because the page calls collection.cached() after each successful create. Works correctly today, but if an offline-created item is synced later, the local id may differ from the server id — verify reconciliation logic if you extend this.

WallBoard.jsx is 1517 lines including 7 inline editor dialogs + bottom nav + main page. File-size guideline (>700 lines) is violated. Recommend splitting each editor (HeroEditor, MessageEditor, GoalEditor, CountdownEditor, AchievementEditor, NoteEditor, FamilyEventEditor, WallSettingsDialog) into /pages/wall/editors/*.jsx for maintainability.
server.py uses @app.on_event('startup') (deprecated in FastAPI 0.99+) — migrate to the lifespan context manager. Not urgent but will warn on newer FastAPI.
The 'previous photo' and 'next photo' carousel buttons use a rotated X icon as a hack instead of a proper chevron. Cosmetic.

Hero photo deletion sends hero_photo: null which the Pydantic WallSettingsUpdate filters out (`v is not None`), so the photo is never actually cleared on the backend. Reproduce: edit hero -> Remove photo -> Save -> reload — old photo will reappear from server. Fix: change the update logic in update_wall_settings to use payload.model_dump(exclude_unset=True) instead of filtering out None, or accept a sentinel.
seed_data_creation: Created and cleaned up TEST_ prefixed wall items (goals, countdown, notes, family-events, achievements, photos). Reset wall_settings hero_title/subtitle/message_text to defaults. DB is clean after testing.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All Wall Board endpoints + UI verified working. test_wall_board.py is the regression suite. Only known semantic issue: PUT /api/wall/settings cannot null-out hero_photo because None values are filtered server-side (excluded from $set). If next iteration tests photo removal flow, expect that bug to surface.
rca of the issue: First persistence-after-reload assertion failed because the test only waited 2.5s after page reload. WallBoard renders synchronously from empty localStorage cache then asynchronously fetches from /api. Increasing the wait to 5s confirmed data is correctly persisted in backend and re-rendered. Not a product bug — backend GET responses already contain the data.
